title: cinna phleoides
intro: Cinna phleoides, also known as sweet woodruff or wild baby's breath, is a perennial plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve anxiety and insomnia. The health benefits of cinna phleoides include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve heart health, and reduce the risk of certain cancers. In herbal preparations, cinna phleoides is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in the production of sweet-smelling potpourri and fragrances.
